name: Jävre
description: =Allmänt= Boulderområde på Högberget, Jävre. Jävre ligger mellan Skellefteå och Piteå. Boulderområdet utvecklades av Luleklättrarna, framförallt Magnus Eriksson och Marcus Lagerkvist (??) inför tävlingen NBM 2005. I dagsläget finns runt 100 boulderproblem och de flesta enklare givna linjerna är gjorda. Framförallt lockar området 'Higball heaven' med sina fina linjer på lätt vattenpolerad granit. =Vägbeskrivning= Högberget ligger i Jävre, 60 km norr om Skellefteå och 25 km söder om Piteå. Från söder kör E4 norrut. Passera småbåtshamnen och turiststationen på vänster sida om E4. Sväng av vägen vid trafikplats Jävre S. Kör över viadukten och ta höger i den lilla rondellen. Från Norr följ E4 söderut. Sväng av vägen åt höger vid trafi kplats Jävre S och följ vägen rakt fram genom den lilla rondellen. Efter rondellen - följ vägen ca 250 m in mot bostadsområdet tills en grusväg med en bom blir synlig på vänster sida. En skylt med texten Arkeologstig visar på rätt väg. Sväng vänster över den lilla bron och följ grusvägen (håll vänster) 2 km tills en grillplats blir synlig till vänster om vägen. Parkera bilen på den öppna ytan och de första blocken (Introstenarna) är synliga en bit upp i backen. =Sektorer=

description.text:
=General= Bouldering area on Högberget in Jävre, which lies between Skellefteå and Piteå. The area was developed by climbers from Luleå, particularly Magnus Eriksson and Marcus Lagerkvist (??), ahead of the NBM 2005 competition. There are currently around 100 boulder problems, and most of the obvious easier lines have been climbed. The main attraction is the ‘Highball Heaven’ sector, with its fine lines on smooth, water-polished granite.

=Directions= Högberget is in Jävre, 60 km north of Skellefteå and 25 km south of Piteå. From the south, follow the E4 north. Pass the marina and tourist centre on the left-hand side of the E4. Take the ‘Jävre S’ exit, cross the flyover and turn right at the small roundabout. From the north, follow the E4 south. Take the ‘Jävre S’ exit on the right and continue straight across the small roundabout. After the roundabout, follow the road towards the residential area for about 250 m until a gated gravel road appears on the left. A sign reading ‘Arkeologstig’ marks the correct road. Turn left across the small bridge and follow the gravel road, keeping left, for 2 km until a barbecue area appears on the left. Park in the open area. The first boulders (Introstenarna) can be seen a short way up the slope.

=Sectors=
Climbs the arete left of "Silversurfaren - 103".
The lowest boulder in the area? Sit start and scramble up. On the back of the same boulder as 123 - Ass Bounce.
On the way from Toppen to Highball Heaven, following the markers. On the arete.
Traverse from left to right. Marked starting holds.
Straight up at the number. Easier for tall climbers.
Sit start. Finish with Aurora. Grade uncertain!
Straight up! An incredible line!
Start directly below the number on two tiny holds beneath the roof (the nose). Then straight up.
Do not mantle out left. Follow the arete all the way up.
Straight up. The crack (107) is excluded.
The large crack.
**PROJECT**
You may jump from the ground if you feel like it. It can also be started with the hands established.
Sit start with both hands on the large hold low on the left. Climb up and finish with the original jump start. The detached rock is, of course, excluded.
Mantle at the arrow furthest right on the boulder.
Grade uncertain. Mantle at the arrow.
The arete is excluded.
Start with both hands on the large sloper. Follow the arete and do NOT mantle out right!
The arete is excluded.
Up the middle of the face.
Establish on the slopers and mantle slightly right of the number.
Low start with both hands on the undercut by the number. Then battle your way up and mantle.
Start with both hands on the lower rail. Then simply mantle up.
The problem is on the arete to the right of Imse vimse, problem no. 1. Jump start to the sloper. A little morpho.
Mantle at the number.
Jump start straight up at the number.
Traverse and finish with no. 8. Marked starting holds.
The arete.
Mantle, with a hanging start from the rock.
Traverse and finish with no. 15.
The arete.
Start in the crack directly in front of the number. The arete on the right is excluded.
The ledge on the left is excluded.
The problem is just below the barbecue area, down towards Highball Heaven. Follow the arete over the lip and mantle up.
The problem is above the barbecue area if you follow baz towards the ravine. It starts beneath the roof on the left-hand side.
Standing start on good holds, though you need to get your feet up. Start on the marked holds, one on either side of the arete.
On the boulder marked in the guide in the baz area. The problem is right of the arete and the problem chip.
On the boulder marked in the guide in the baz area. The problem is right of the arete, after Butch.
On the boulder marked in the guide in the baz area. The problem is furthest right on the arete. Start on two crimps, one on either side of the arete. A slightly nasty landing.
Finish with 52. A low problem in the Ravinen area.
Mantle.
In the Ravinen area.
In the Ravinen area.
In the Ravinen area.
In the Ravinen area.
In the Ravinen area. Start on the large flake.
In the Ravinen area.
In the Ravinen area.
In the Ravinen area.
Start with the left hand in an undercling beneath the roof. Anything goes to the top, followed by a lovely mantle.
Straight up the right-hand side of the arete.
Start on the jug beneath the roof, climb out towards the lip and mantle where it feels natural.
Start on the jug beneath the roof and climb straight up.
Standing start on the small rock beneath the roof. Climb straight up and mantle.
